title: Vachellia elatior
taxonomy: Vachellia elatior is classified under the kingdom Plantae, with a more specific classification in the following order: Vachellia elatior falls under the division Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Fabales, family Fabaceae, and genus Vachellia. The family Fabaceae is a large group of plants commonly known as legumes, which are characterized by their fruit and flower structures. Understanding the classification of Vachellia elatior provides insights into its evolutionary history and relationships with other organisms.

intro: Vachellia elatior, also known as the giant mimosa or sensitive plant, is a tropical tree species native to Central and South America.
